ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT PAUL LANCASTER WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING MATERIALS DISTURBED BY DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFFS' INTERROGATORY RESPONSES STATED THAT MR. HUNTER WILL TESTIFY THAT MR. LANCASTER WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING MATERIALS DISTURBED BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT MR. HUNTER WAS UNABLE TO PROVIDE ADMISSIBLE TESTIFY ABOUT THOSE FACTS. (SEE PURPORTED UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS 4, 8, AND 15-19). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2011 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Carmichael

RULING:
DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Exxon Mobil Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Paul Lancaster was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses stated that Mr. Hunter will testify that Mr. Lancaster was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Defendant's separate statement failed to demonstrate that Mr. Hunter was unable to provide admissible testify about those facts. (See purported undisputed material facts 4, 8, and 15-19). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077. = (220/HEK)
